subject: Civics
chapter: Chapter: Parliament and the Making of Laws

key_points: Parliament is the highest law-making body in India
It represents the people of the country
Parliament makes laws and discusses national issues
The Parliament of India has two houses: Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha
Members of Parliament are elected by the people
Parliament controls the government and ensures accountability
examples: Members of Parliament discuss important issues such as education and health
Lok Sabha members are directly elected by citizens
Parliament debates new laws before passing them
The government must answer questions raised by Members of Parliament
formulas: Indian Parliament → Lok Sabha + Rajya Sabha
Law making → Bill → Debate → Approval → Law
exam_tips: Remember the two houses of Parliament: Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha
Explain the role of Parliament in making laws
Mention how Parliament represents the people
Write about Parliament's role in controlling the government
